prompt: I would like a detailed analysis of the Saint Seiya franchise (anime/manga). The analysis should be structured around the different classes of armor (Cloths, Scales, Surplices, God Robes, etc.), such as Bronze Saints, Silver Saints, Gold Saints, Marina Generals, Specters, God Warriors, etc. For each significant character within these categories, provide details on their power level, signature techniques, key appearances/story arcs, and final outcome/fate within the series.
article:
The cleanest way to analyze *Saint Seiya* is **armor-class first, continuity second**.

Because the franchise has multiple official continuities, I’ll use this rule throughout:

- **Baseline manga line:** Masami Kurumada’s original manga + **Next Dimension** (which ended on July 4, 2024) [[9]](https://www.animenewsnetwork.com/news/2024-07-01/saint-seiya-next-dimension-manga-ends-on-july-4/.212642)[[10]](https://kurumadapro.com/column/article_105.html).
- **Classic anime line:** 1986 TV anime + anime-only additions like **Asgard** + **Hades Sanctuary / Inferno / Elysion** OVAs [[12]](https://lineup.toei-anim.co.jp/ja/ova/seiya_12/story/)[[13]](https://lineup.toei-anim.co.jp/ja/tv/seiya_12/episode/1/)[[124]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_The_Hades_Chapter)[[125]](https://www.saintseiya-anime.com/en/history/).
- **Official spin-off lines:** **Episode G**, **The Lost Canvas**, **Saintia Shō** / **Saintia Shō Memories** (with *Memories* ending on March 19, 2026), etc. [[17]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Episode.G)[[22]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_The_Lost_Canvas)[[23]](https://www.animenewsnetwork.com/news/2026-02-25/saint-seiya-saintia-sho-memories-manga-miniseries-to-end-on-march-19/.234469)[[1]](https://www.akitashoten.co.jp/red/backnumber)
- **Official alternate anime/reboot lines:** **Omega**, **Soul of Gold**, and the CG **Knights of the Zodiac / Battle for Sanctuary** reboot [[25]](https://lineup.toei-anim.co.jp/ja/tv/seiya_omega/episode/)[[2]](https://corp.toei-anim.co.jp/ja/press/press-4250291089398818023.html)[[4]](https://corp.toei-anim.co.jp/en/press/press3640146825760962351.html)[[162]](https://www.saintseiya-kotz.com/introduction.html)[[163]](https://www.saintseiya-kotz.com/story.html).

That matters because **deaths, resurrections, power portrayals, and even master-disciple relationships can change by continuity**. So below, “fate” always means **fate within the relevant continuity**, not one universal franchise fate.

A second caveat: the franchise has **hundreds of armored characters**. To keep this useful, I focus on the **significant names in each armor class**—the main Bronze/Silver/Gold Saints, the seven Marina Generals, the key God Warriors, Hades/Hypnos/Thanatos, the Judges, and the most notable Specters—plus the major spin-off reinterpretations. Fine-grained details in some spin-offs come from fan encyclopedic sources and should be treated more cautiously than official Toei/Akita material; I flag that where needed [[67]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Saint_Seiya_characters)[[121]](https://tvtropes.org/pmwiki/pmwiki.php/Characters/SaintSeiyaTheLostCanvasUnderworld).

## 1) How the armor systems work

### Cloths (Athena’s Saints)
Athena’s **88 Cloths** correspond to the constellations and are divided into **48 Bronze, 24 Silver, 12 Gold**, plus Athena’s own divine armor outside the 88 [[29]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Cloths). Cloths amplify the wearer’s **Cosmo** rather than functioning as independent power sources; in other words, rank matters, but **Cosmo output matters more** [[29]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Cloths). Stronger Cloths generally cover more of the body, and “dead” Cloths can be revived with a great amount of a Saint’s blood [[29]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Cloths).

### God Cloths / Kamui
These are the transcendental forms above normal Cloths: near-total body coverage, enormous durability, and effectively divine protection [[39]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/God_Cloths)[[41]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Kamui). In anime continuity, **Soul of Gold** gives the Gold Saints temporary God Cloth forms through Athena’s blood [[2]](https://corp.toei-anim.co.jp/ja/press/press-4250291089398818023.html)[[74]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Soul_of_Gold).

### Black Cloths
Recognized as dark mirror counterparts to familiar Bronze-line Cloths (Black Pegasus, Black Dragon, etc.), but the retrieved sources are thin on formal in-universe mechanics [[42]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Cloths)[[43]](http://www.saintseiyapedia.com/wiki/Cat%C3%A9gorie:Saint_Cloth_Series).

### Steel Cloths
Anime-origin technological “Cloths,” created by **Dr. Asamori** and the **Graad Foundation**, rather than divine relics [[35]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Steel_Cloths)[[44]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Basic_Steel_Cloth).

### Scales (Poseidon’s Mariners)
These are the armors of Poseidon’s mariners and are made primarily of **Orichalcum**, described as the strongest metal on Earth in the franchise’s lore [[30]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Scales)[[40]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Orichalcum). The Scales predate Athena’s Cloth system in mythic backstory and were one reason Athena needed to create the Saints [[84]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Mariners).

### Surplices (Hades’ Specters)
The **108 Specters** wear Surplices, Hades’ equivalent of Cloths [[31]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Surplices). They are associated with evil stars and mythic monsters rather than constellations [[31]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Surplices). Unlike Cloths, Surplices are not clearly divided into Bronze/Silver/Gold-like material tiers [[31]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Surplices). Hades, Hypnos, and Thanatos have divine-blood-soaked Surplices, but these are still explicitly not true Kamui/God Cloths [[31]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Surplices).

### God Robes / God Warrior armor
The Asgardian equivalent, worn by Odin’s **God Warriors** [[32]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/God_Robes). In practice there are two big branches:
- the **classic anime-only Asgard arc**, and
- **Soul of Gold**, which revisits Asgard with a new God Warrior roster [[106]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/es/wiki/Arco_de_Asgard)[[86]](https://www.saintseiya-gold.com/en/story.html).

### Other major variants
- **Chronotectors**: Pallasite armor in **Omega** [[33]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Pallasites)[[56]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Pallasites_Second_Class).
- **Leaves**: Eris’ Dryad armor in **Saintia Shō** [[34]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Dryades)[[58]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Saintia_Sho)[[59]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Eris_(Saintia_Sh%C5%8D)).
- **Cloth Stones**: Omega’s jewel-based Cloth manifestation system [[45]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Clothstones)[[36]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Saint_Seiya_Omega).

## 2) Power scaling: what “strong” means in Saint Seiya

A useful franchise-wide hierarchy is:

- **Bronze Saints**: nominally the lowest rank, but the main heroes constantly exceed rank through extreme Cosmo bursts.
- **Silver Saints**: mid/high officers, often superior to ordinary Bronze by armor class.
- **Gold Saints**: the elite twelve; in *Episode G*, Gold Saints are explicitly framed as masters of the **Seventh Sense** and capable of **light-speed movement** [[66]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Saint_Seiya_Episode.G_characters)[[17]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Episode.G).
- **Marina Generals / God Warriors / Judges of Hell**: the top officers of rival factions; broadly comparable as “arc boss” elites, though direct cross-faction scaling is continuity-dependent [[84]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Mariners)[[90]](https://tvtropes.org/pmwiki/pmwiki.php/Anime/SaintSeiyaSoulOfGold)[[120]](https://tvtropes.org/pmwiki/pmwiki.php/Characters/SaintSeiyaSpecters).
- **Twin gods / Poseidon / Hades / Loki / similar divine beings**: true divine tier.
- **God Cloth / Kamui states**: moments when Saints temporarily bridge toward divine-level combat [[39]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/God_Cloths)[[41]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Kamui)[[74]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Soul_of_Gold).

Important franchise rule: **armor rank does not automatically decide the fight**. Cloths amplify Cosmo; they do not replace it. Even a lower-ranked Saint can hurt a higher-ranked one if the Cosmo gap is big enough [[29]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Cloths).

---

## 3) Athena’s Cloth users

## Bronze Saints

### The five core Bronze Saints

| Character | Armor / standing | Signature techniques | Key arcs | Fate / final status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Pegasus Seiya** | Pegasus Bronze Saint; repeatedly exceeds Bronze level and at times uses the **Sagittarius Gold Cloth**; franchise central hero [[65]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya)[[67]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Saint_Seiya_characters) | **Pegasus Meteor Fist**, **Pegasus Comet Fist**, Rolling Crash | Galaxian Wars, Black Saints, Sanctuary, Poseidon, Hades; in **Next Dimension**, his condition drives the entire plot [[65]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya)[[68]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Next_Dimension) | At the end of the original series, he survives but is left under **Hades’ sword curse**, a death sentence that Athena tries to undo in **Next Dimension** [[68]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Next_Dimension) |
| **Dragon Shiryu** | Dragon Bronze Saint; the Dragon Cloth is explicitly described as having the strongest fist and shield among Bronze Cloths [[67]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Saint_Seiya_characters) | **Rozan Shō Ryū Ha** and related Dragon/Rozan attacks | Galaxian Wars, Silver Saint battles, Sanctuary, Poseidon, Hades; repeatedly uses the **Libra Gold Cloth** [[67]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Saint_Seiya_characters)[[65]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya) | Survives the original line; in **Next Dimension**, he and Hyoga go back to the 18th-century Holy War to help save Seiya [[67]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Saint_Seiya_characters)[[68]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Next_Dimension) |
| **Cygnus Hyoga** | Cygnus Bronze Saint; ice specialist capable of approaching/attaining absolute-zero-style attacks [[67]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Saint_Seiya_characters) | **Diamond Dust**, **Aurora Thunder Attack**, **Aurora Execution** | Sanctuary, Poseidon, Hades; notably fights Kraken Isaac in the Poseidon arc [[67]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Saint_Seiya_characters)[[65]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya) | Survives into **Next Dimension**, where he joins the time-travel rescue effort for Seiya [[67]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Saint_Seiya_characters)[[68]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Next_Dimension) |
| **Andromeda Shun** | Andromeda Bronze Saint; one of the most dangerous Bronze Saints when fully unleashed [[67]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Saint_Seiya_characters) | **Nebula Chain**, **Nebula Storm** | Sanctuary, Poseidon, Hades; in Hades arc he is revealed as **Hades’ intended vessel** [[67]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Saint_Seiya_characters)[[65]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya) | Freed from possession, continues to Elysium, later travels with Athena into the 18th century in **Next Dimension** [[67]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Saint_Seiya_characters)[[68]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Next_Dimension) |
| **Phoenix Ikki** | Phoenix Bronze Saint; the most individually overwhelming of the five in raw intimidation and comeback factor [[67]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Saint_Seiya_characters) | **Phoenix Genma Ken**, **Hō Yoku Tenshō** | Black Saints, Sanctuary, Poseidon, Hades; often arrives late to turn losing battles [[67]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Saint_Seiya_characters)[[65]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya) | Survives the original line; in **Next Dimension**, defeats Artemis’ agents and later enters the 18th-century war [[67]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Saint_Seiya_characters)[[68]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Next_Dimension) |

### What makes the Bronze Five special
They are **formally low-rank**, but narratively they are miracle-producing outliers. The franchise repeatedly uses them to prove that **Cosmo > nominal armor class** [[29]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Cloths). Seiya, Shiryu, Hyoga, Shun, and Ikki all cross class boundaries at crucial moments—especially when borrowing Gold Cloths or awakening higher Cosmo states [[65]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya)[[67]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Saint_Seiya_characters).

### Important classic/anime differences for the Bronze Five
Two are especially worth noting:

- In the **anime**, **Hyoga** is trained by the **Crystal Saint**, an anime-original intermediary, whereas in the manga his training is tied directly to Camus [[67]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Saint_Seiya_characters).
- In the **anime**, **Shun’s master** is renamed **Albiore** instead of Daidalos [[67]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Saint_Seiya_characters).

Those are small on paper but important if you are comparing mentor lineages or power progression across manga vs anime.

### Other notable Bronze Saints
The secondary Bronze group—**Jabu, Nachi, Ban, Geki, Ichi**—remains important mostly as support or foil characters in the classic era and also appears in **Saintia Shō** [[71]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Saintia_Sh%C5%8D). They are significant to the franchise, but the main narrative and power escalations overwhelmingly center on the core five.

---

## Silver Saints

Silver Saints tend to serve three functions: **mid-bosses**, **mentors**, or **fallen/ambiguous officers**.

### Eagle Marin
- **Class:** Silver Saint, one of the most important mentor figures around Seiya [[71]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Saintia_Sh%C5%8D)[[166]](https://www.saintseiya-kotz.com/character.html).
- **Power level:** clearly above ordinary Bronze by rank; across continuities she is portrayed as a serious field Saint and high-end trainer.
- **Signature style:** eagle-themed strikes and high-mobility combat.
- **Key arcs:** Seiya’s training/master relationship in the classic story; appears again in later lines including **Saintia Shō** and is tied into the orbit of **Tōma** in **Next Dimension** [[68]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Next_Dimension)[[71]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Saintia_Sh%C5%8D).
- **Fate:** generally survives and remains a recurring ally/mentor figure.

### Ophiuchus Shaina
- **Class:** Silver Saint [[71]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Saintia_Sh%C5%8D).
- **Power level:** one of the most prominent active Silver Saints in the core era.
- **Signature style:** serpent-based martial offense.
- **Key arcs:** early antagonism toward Seiya, later repeated alliance; appears in **Saintia Shō** as part of the classic cast crossover [[71]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Saintia_Sh%C5%8D).
- **Fate:** generally survives as a continuing Athena ally.

### Cepheus Daidalos / anime Albiore
- **Class:** Silver Saint; master of Shun [[67]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Saint_Seiya_characters).
- **Power level:** elite teacher among Silvers.
- **Key arcs:** backstory/training role for Shun; important mainly because the **manga/anime naming differs** [[67]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Saint_Seiya_characters).
- **Fate:** dies in the classic story’s Silver-Saint conflict phase.

### Crateris Suikyō (Next Dimension)
- **Class:** originally **Crateris Silver Saint**, later defects and becomes **Garuda Judge of Hell** [[68]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Next_Dimension).
- **Power level:** extremely high for a former Silver; his promotion to Judge-level enemy status tells you how dangerous he is.
- **Key arcs:** one of the most important **Next Dimension** antagonists; former master of Pegasus Tenma and childhood associate of Shion and Dohko [[68]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Next_Dimension).
- **Fate:** explicitly dies during the 18th-century conflict; Pandora gives Vermeer further orders after Suikyō’s death [[135]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Pandora_(_ND_)).

### Ophiuchus Odysseus (special case)
- **Original standing:** in **Next Dimension**, he begins as a legendary **Silver Saint** famed for miraculous healing [[68]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Next_Dimension).
- **Transformation:** later returns as the hidden/forbidden **Ophiuchus Gold Saint**, making him one of the most dramatic rank escalations in the franchise [[68]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Next_Dimension).
- **Narrative importance:** he is less a normal Silver and more a continuity-shaking anomaly in the 18th-century story.

---

## Gold Saints

Gold Saints are the elite face of the franchise. In *Episode G*, they are explicitly described as masters of the Seventh Sense and capable of light-speed movement [[66]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Saint_Seiya_Episode.G_characters)[[17]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Episode.G). In the original story they begin as the great obstacle of the Twelve Temples, but by the end of the Hades era they are also the highest non-divine defenders of Athena.

### Shion: the “13th classic Gold” in practical importance
Even though discussions of the twelve Gold Saints usually mean the 20th-century Zodiac guardians, **Shion** is too important to omit:

- **Role:** former Aries Gold Saint and previous Holy War survivor with Dohko; in some retellings/adaptations he is also Pope [[166]](https://www.saintseiya-kotz.com/character.html)[[68]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Next_Dimension).
- **Power level:** top-tier Gold/Sanctuary authority.
- **Key significance:** he anchors the historical continuity between the previous Holy War and the modern era.
- **Fate:** continuity-specific; survives the earlier war, but later status varies by line and adaptation.

### Why Gold Saints dominate spin-offs
Two big spin-offs elevate them even further:

- **Episode G** makes **Aiolia** the lead and turns the Gold Saints into protagonist-gods-in-all-but-name against Titans; Mu gets a huge expansion as telekinetic/repair genius, Aldebaran as raw-force wall, and Saga as both hero and danger [[17]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Episode.G)[[66]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Saint_Seiya_Episode.G_characters).
- **The Lost Canvas** turns the previous Holy War’s Gold Saints into a tragic ensemble. Several receive explicit sacrificial fates:
  - **Pisces Albafica** dies protecting Sanctuary and a nearby town [[22]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_The_Lost_Canvas)
  - **Virgo Asmita** dies creating the rosary that seals Specters [[22]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_The_Lost_Canvas)
  - **Scorpio Kardia** and **Aquarius Dégel** die on their special mission [[22]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_The_Lost_Canvas)
  - **Sagittarius Sisyphos** dies in battle [[22]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_The_Lost_Canvas)
  - **Gemini Defteros** dies after giving Dohko Athena’s Cloth [[22]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_The_Lost_Canvas)
  - **Shion** and **Dohko** remain the canonical survivor pair that link back to the classic timeline premise [[22]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_The_Lost_Canvas)[[68]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Next_Dimension)

That is one of the biggest tonal differences between classic Gold Saints and Lost Canvas Gold Saints: in *Lost Canvas*, the Golds are not just elite bosses or mentors—they are the primary **heroic casualty class** [[22]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_The_Lost_Canvas)[[83]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Saint_Seiya:_The_Lost_Canvas_characters).

## Other Athena-side armored groups

### Saintias (*Saintia Shō*)
*Saintia Shō* introduces the **Saintias**, Athena’s personal maidens/bodyguards—a distinct female Saint order [[71]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Saintia_Sh%C5%8D). The manga ended in 2021, with the final volume in 2022, and *Saintia Shō Memories* ended on March 19, 2026 [[23]](https://www.animenewsnetwork.com/news/2026-02-25/saint-seiya-saintia-sho-memories-manga-miniseries-to-end-on-march-19/.234469)[[1]](https://www.akitashoten.co.jp/red/backnumber).

Key Saintias from the retrieved material:

- **Equuleus Shōko**  
  - Main heroine, central Saintia of the spin-off [[71]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Saintia_Sh%C5%8D)[[80]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Equuleus_Sh%C5%8Dko)  
  - Power level: protagonist-tier within the spin-off, but not framed as classic Gold-tier  
  - Role: protects Athena against Eris and Dryads  
  - Fate: survives through the spin-off’s endgame, though the retrieved source set is light on her exact final-state details [[71]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Saintia_Sh%C5%8D)[[23]](https://www.animenewsnetwork.com/news/2026-02-25/saint-seiya-saintia-sho-memories-manga-miniseries-to-end-on-march-19/.234469)

- **Equuleus Kyōko**  
  - Shōko’s sister and one of the most important emotional anchors of the series [[81]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Equuleus_Ky%C5%8Dko)  
  - Role/fate: heavily tied to Eris-vessel drama; continuity-specific and central to the plot [[71]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Saintia_Sh%C5%8D)[[81]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Equuleus_Ky%C5%8Dko)

- **Delphinus Mii**  
  - Saintia ally associated with water-based combat [[82]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Delphinus_Mii)  
  - Role: one of the principal Saintia companions around Shōko  
  - Fate: not fully detailed in the retrieved source set [[82]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Delphinus_Mii)

The important structural point is that **Saintias are an official Athena-side armored order**, not just side characters [[71]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Saintia_Sh%C5%8D).

### Steel Saints
Anime-origin, technology-based pseudo-Saint order created by human science rather than divine relics [[35]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Steel_Cloths)[[44]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Basic_Steel_Cloth). Important franchise conceptually, but not central to the manga/anime power hierarchy compared with Bronze/Silver/Gold.

### Omega’s new generation
In **Omega**, the Cloth system is reworked through **Cloth Stones** and elemental Cosmo [[45]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Clothstones)[[36]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Saint_Seiya_Omega)[[75]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya_Omega). The new lead Bronze group includes:
- **Pegasus Kōga**
- **Aquila Yuna**
- **Dragon Ryūhō**
- **Lionet Sōma**
- **Wolf Haruto**
- later **Orion Eden** and **Subaru** [[75]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya_Omega)

Most important continuity note: **Seiya is no longer just the Pegasus Bronze hero—he becomes a legendary older Saint and one of Athena’s Gold Saints in this continuity** [[75]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya_Omega). That is one of the franchise’s clearest examples of why continuity labels matter.

## 4) Poseidon’s Scales: Mariners and Marina Generals

Poseidon’s mariners predate the Saint system in mythic backstory and originally overwhelmed surface warriors, forcing Athena to create the Saints and Cloths [[84]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Mariners). Their elite officers are the **seven Marina Generals**, each tied to one of the sea pillars [[84]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Mariners).

### Poseidon
- **Armor:** Poseidon Scale [[88]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Poseidon_Scale)[[89]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Poseidon)
- **Power level:** true god-tier; supreme marine faction ruler [[89]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Poseidon)
- **Key arcs:** Poseidon/Undersea Temple saga; in wider continuity he remains a recurring divine force [[89]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Poseidon)[[65]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya)
- **Fate:** defeated/sealed at the end of the Poseidon war in the classic line [[65]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya)

### The seven classic Marina Generals

### Mermaid Thetis
- **Rank:** lower-ranking Marina, servant/assistant to Poseidon and the Generals [[96]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Mermaid_Thetis)
- **Importance:** shows that Poseidon’s marine army is not just the seven Generals; there are subordinate mariners too [[84]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Mariners)[[96]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Mermaid_Thetis)
- **Fate:** secondary survivorship varies by version, but she remains one of the best-known non-General mariners.

### How strong are Marina Generals?
They are the top officers of Poseidon’s side and are clearly treated as **elite arc bosses**, but exact cross-scaling with Gold Saints varies by continuity and medium [[84]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Mariners)[[90]](https://tvtropes.org/pmwiki/pmwiki.php/Anime/SaintSeiyaSoulOfGold). In broad narrative terms, they sit below Poseidon himself and below true divine-tier endgame states, but well above normal Saints.

### Major spin-off / alternate versions
- **Lost Canvas:** the Poseidon-linked focus narrows to **Sea Dragon Unity**, who wields Poseidon’s power in orichalcum and later **renounces Marina status** [[97]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Sea_Dragon_Unity).
- **Omega-era derivative material:** retains **Sorrento** and even adds unique marine concepts like the **Triton Scale** in game-adjacent material [[84]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Mariners)[[87]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Triton_Scale).

## 5) God Robes / God Warriors (Asgard)

This is the biggest case where **anime continuity matters**. The classic **Asgard arc** is anime-only, not part of Kurumada’s original manga [[73]](https://tvtropes.org/pmwiki/pmwiki.php/Manga/SaintSeiya)[[106]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/es/wiki/Arco_de_Asgard). Later, **Soul of Gold** revisits Asgard with a new roster and a different premise [[2]](https://corp.toei-anim.co.jp/ja/press/press-4250291089398818023.html)[[86]](https://www.saintseiya-gold.com/en/story.html).

## A. Classic anime Asgard God Warriors

The classic seven are awakened under the corruption of **Hilda** by the **Nibelungen Ring**: **Thor, Fenrir, Hagen, Alberich, Mime, Syd, Siegfried** [[106]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/es/wiki/Arco_de_Asgard).

| Character | Standing | Signature style | Key role | Fate / outcome |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Siegfried of Dubhe** | Strongest/leader figure among the God Warriors [[111]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/es/wiki/Siegfried_de_Dubhe) | Dragon-hero style overwhelming offense/defense | Final major warrior of Asgard; initially loyal to Hilda | After learning Hilda was manipulated, turns against the plot and dies in tragic redemption, confronting Sorrento/Poseidon’s influence [[107]](https://tvtropes.org/pmwiki/pmwiki.php/Characters/SaintSeiyaAsgardSagaCharacters) |
| **Syd of Mizar** | One of the strongest God Warriors [[108]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Merak_Beta_H%C3%A4gen)[[110]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/es/wiki/Syd_de_Mizar) | Wolf/twin-shadow style assault | High-end enforcer of Hilda’s regime | Falls in the Asgard war |
| **Hagen of Merak** | Explicitly grouped among the top three God Warriors; controls both heat and cold [[108]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Merak_Beta_H%C3%A4gen) | Fire-and-ice style attacks | Personal, emotionally driven Asgard fighter | Defeated in the Asgard arc |
| **Alberich of Megrez** | The schemer of the group; notably not purely loyal [[107]](https://tvtropes.org/pmwiki/pmwiki.php/Characters/SaintSeiyaAsgardSagaCharacters)[[112]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/es/wiki/Alberich_de_Megrez) | Nature/illusion/trap style | Internal opportunist trying to profit from the crisis | Defeated; serves as the “corrupt God Warrior” counterpart to the more tragic loyalists |
| **Fenrir of Alioth** | Savage/noble-blood warrior chosen by Hilda [[113]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/es/wiki/Fenrir_de_Alioth) | Wild beast/claw style | Feral guardian archetype | Defeated in Asgard |
| **Thor of Phecda** | Classic brute-force God Warrior [[106]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/es/wiki/Arco_de_Asgard) | Hammer/power-based offense | Early arc powerhouse obstacle | Defeated |
| **Mime** | Elegant, music-based God Warrior [[106]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/es/wiki/Arco_de_Asgard) | Harp/string sonic combat | One of Asgard’s most memorable technical fighters | Defeated in the Asgard arc |

### Why the classic God Warriors matter
They are a full rival elite class parallel to the Marina Generals: **seven mythic officers under a divine sovereign figure**. Their tragedy is that they are less outright evil than many classic enemies—they are largely **misled or manipulated** by Hilda’s corruption [[107]](https://tvtropes.org/pmwiki/pmwiki.php/Characters/SaintSeiyaAsgardSagaCharacters).

## B. Soul of Gold’s God Warriors

In **Soul of Gold**, the twelve Gold Saints—who should have vanished after the Wall of Lamentation—are mysteriously revived in Asgard [[2]](https://corp.toei-anim.co.jp/ja/press/press-4250291089398818023.html). There they face a **new generation of God Warriors** under the Yggdrasil/Loki plot [[86]](https://www.saintseiya-gold.com/en/story.html)[[74]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Soul_of_Gold).

### The resurrected Gold Saints in Soul of Gold
Before the new God Warriors, one crucial note: **Soul of Gold** is also a Gold-Saint showcase. Aiolia, Mu, Aldebaran, Saga, Deathmask, Aphrodite, Shaka, Milo, Camus, Shura, Dohko, and Aiolos are all active; Athena’s blood awakens **God Cloths** in them, and after Loki is defeated they disappear again [[74]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Soul_of_Gold).

### Soul of Gold’s final status
After Loki is defeated:
- the Gold Saints’ borrowed lives expire and they disappear again [[74]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Soul_of_Gold)
- **Lyfia** becomes Odin’s representative [[74]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Soul_of_Gold)
- **Frodi** and **Sigmund** help rebuild Asgard [[74]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Soul_of_Gold)

That ending makes *Soul of Gold* both a celebration of the Gold Saints and a second tragic Asgard cycle.

## 6) Hades’ Surplices: Specters, Judges, and Underworld gods

The Hades faction is the most structurally elaborate enemy force in the franchise:
- **Hades** at the top
- **Pandora** as chief human/operational commander
- **Hypnos** and **Thanatos** as twin gods
- the **Three Judges of Hell**
- the **108 Specters** in Surplices [[31]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Surplices)[[120]](https://tvtropes.org/pmwiki/pmwiki.php/Characters/SaintSeiyaSpecters)

The anime adaptation of the Hades arc is divided into **Sanctuary**, **Inferno**, and **Elysion** [[124]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_The_Hades_Chapter)[[125]](https://www.saintseiya-anime.com/en/history/).

## A. Supreme tier

### Hades
- **Armor:** divine Hades Surplice [[31]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Surplices)
- **Power level:** top Underworld god; far above ordinary Specters [[128]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Hades)[[68]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Next_Dimension)
- **Key role:** final antagonist of the classic saga; in *Next Dimension* his sword curse on Seiya is the story’s starting crisis [[68]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Next_Dimension)
- **Signature style:** godly darkness/telekinetic and curse authority; exact move names are less well covered in the retrieved source set
- **Fate:** defeated in the classic endgame, but Seiya is left mortally cursed by his sword, which **Next Dimension** attempts to undo [[65]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya)[[68]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Next_Dimension)

### Pandora
- **Standing:** Hades’ right hand; operational commander over Specters and Judges [[120]](https://tvtropes.org/pmwiki/pmwiki.php/Characters/SaintSeiyaSpecters)[[135]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Pandora_(_ND_))
- **Power level:** not Judge-tier in pure combat importance, but enormously important in command authority
- **Key roles:** directs Hades’ war machinery in both classic Hades material and **Next Dimension**; in **Lost Canvas** she is central to the aerial-castle campaign around Alone/Hades [[134]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Alone_(LC))[[135]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Pandora_(_ND_))
- **Fate:** continuity-specific and more tragic/servant-like than triumphal.

### Hypnos
- **Armor:** divine Surplice [[31]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Surplices)
- **Power level:** god-tier beneath Hades but above ordinary Specters [[129]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Hypnos)
- **Signature style:** sleep/manipulation authority
- **Key arcs:** endgame divine obstacle as Athena’s Saints attempt to rescue her from certain death [[129]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Hypnos)[[124]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_The_Hades_Chapter)
- **Fate:** overcome in the Elysium climax when the Saints enter their highest late-series state [[65]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya)

### Thanatos
- **Armor:** divine Surplice [[31]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Surplices)
- **Power level:** god-tier beneath Hades; death personification [[131]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Thanatos)
- **Key arcs:** like Hypnos, a final Elysium barrier [[129]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Hypnos)[[131]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Thanatos)
- **Special note:** in **Lost Canvas**, secondary continuity material portrays him as even more interventionist, able to grant power to Specters and command specific subordinates [[132]](https://thelostcanvas.fandom.com/wiki/Thanatos)
- **Fate:** defeated in the late Hades endgame when the Saints reach god-level resistance [[65]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya)

## B. The Three Judges of Hell

These are Hades’ highest non-divine combat officers.

### Original/classic configuration

|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Wyvern Rhadamanthys** | Judge of Hell, top Surplice commander [[120]](https://tvtropes.org/pmwiki/pmwiki.php/Characters/SaintSeiyaSpecters)[[136]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Wyvern_Rhadamanthys) | One of the most prominent and feared Specters; high commander status is consistent across versions [[120]](https://tvtropes.org/pmwiki/pmwiki.php/Characters/SaintSeiyaSpecters) | Classic Hades conflict; also central in **Lost Canvas** [[136]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Wyvern_Rhadamanthys) | Falls in the wider Underworld war; exact scene-level fate varies by continuity/adaptation emphasis |

| **Garuda Aiacos** | Judge of Hell [[121]](https://tvtropes.org/pmwiki/pmwiki.php/Characters/SaintSeiyaTheLostCanvasUnderworld) | One of the elite three, though less emphasized in the retrieved source set | Original Hades war; also part of the **Lost Canvas** Judge roster [[121]](https://tvtropes.org/pmwiki/pmwiki.php/Characters/SaintSeiyaTheLostCanvasUnderworld) | Defeated during Hades-side collapse |

### How strong are the Judges?
They are the **peak non-divine Surplice users**. Secondary sources in the research repeatedly frame the upper Specter echelon—especially the Judges—as the only Hades soldiers consistently credible against Gold Saints [[117]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Specters)[[120]](https://tvtropes.org/pmwiki/pmwiki.php/Characters/SaintSeiyaSpecters).

### A note on Specter coverage
For many Specters, the retrieved sources give **excellent classification data** (evil star, prison, function, Surplice identity) but **limited reliable move-name or final-fate detail** [[31]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Surplices)[[138]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Papillon_Myu)[[139]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Balron_Lune)[[142]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Sphinx_Pharaoh)[[145]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Lycaon_Phlegyas). So the safest way to read them is:
- **Judges / twin gods / Hades** = strategic core
- **named upper Specters** = memorable boss-tier specialists
- **many others** = function-based underworld obstacles

## 7) Major continuity reinterpretations of Hades’ side

## Next Dimension (Kurumada’s canonical sequel/prequel)

This is the most important Hades-side reinterpretation in the official Kurumada manga line [[9]](https://www.animenewsnetwork.com/news/2024-07-01/saint-seiya-next-dimension-manga-ends-on-july-4/.212642)[[10]](https://kurumadapro.com/column/article_105.html)[[68]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Next_Dimension).

### Pegasus Tenma and the 18th-century Holy War
*Next Dimension* returns to the **previous Holy War 243 years earlier**, where the Hades conflict is re-staged through new Saints and a different Sanctuary lineup [[68]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Next_Dimension).

### Hades-side standouts in Next Dimension

- **Garuda Suikyō**  
  - Formerly **Crateris Silver Saint**, later a Judge-level Specter [[68]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Next_Dimension)  
  - One of the most tragic enemy figures because he began as Athena’s man and Tenma’s master [[68]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Next_Dimension)  
  - Fate: explicitly dead before Pandora’s later Vermeer order sequence [[135]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Pandora_(_ND_))

- **Griffon Vermeer**  
  - One of the three 18th-century Judges [[122]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Griffon_Vermeer)  
  - Leads Specter forces deeper into Sanctuary after Suikyō’s death [[135]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Pandora_(_ND_))  
  - Very important as the replacement-command face of the Hades offensive

- **The 18th-century Hades war structure**  
  Instead of replaying the exact Sanctuary→Inferno→Elysion flow, *Next Dimension* sends Specters **into the Twelve Houses themselves**, turning Sanctuary into an immediate battlefield in the prior Holy War [[68]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Next_Dimension)[[11]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Next_Dimension).

## The Lost Canvas

*The Lost Canvas* is an official spin-off, but not the same continuity as Kurumada’s *Next Dimension* [[22]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_The_Lost_Canvas)[[68]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Next_Dimension).

### Alone as Hades’ vessel
- **Alone** is the Hades vessel of this continuity [[134]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Alone_(LC))
- He works from a sky castle created by Pandora and Hades’ forces and develops the apocalyptic **Lost Canvas** painting [[134]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Alone_(LC))
- This makes the *Lost Canvas* Hades side feel more **mythic, artistic, and apocalyptic**, less strictly prison-by-prison than the classic Underworld march [[134]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Alone_(LC))

### Lost Canvas Hades-side core
- **Pandora**
- **Rhadamanthys**
- **Minos**
- **Aiacos**
- **Hypnos**
- **Thanatos**
- expanded Specter formations and sub-groups [[121]](https://tvtropes.org/pmwiki/pmwiki.php/Characters/SaintSeiyaTheLostCanvasUnderworld)[[136]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Wyvern_Rhadamanthys)[[137]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Griffon_Minos)

### Lost Canvas tone difference
If classic Hades is a long descent through death-realms, *Lost Canvas* is more of a **grand tragic war epic** between equally foregrounded hero and villain ensembles [[22]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_The_Lost_Canvas)[[121]](https://tvtropes.org/pmwiki/pmwiki.php/Characters/SaintSeiyaTheLostCanvasUnderworld).

## Dark Wing

A modern official spin-off and a major reinterpretation of Surplices [[118]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Dark_Wing).

- Main premise: the student **Shōichirō** dies and awakens in the land of the dead as **Wyvern**, one of the three Judges of Hades [[118]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Dark_Wing)[[154]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Dark_Wing)
- Many classmates/teachers become either Athena’s fighters or Hades’ Specters [[118]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Dark_Wing)
- This line modernizes the Surplice/Judge concept into an isekai-style war drama while keeping Pandora, Judge offices, and Surplice identity central [[118]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Dark_Wing)

It is still ongoing in the retrieved material, so no definitive final fates yet [[118]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Dark_Wing).

## 9) Cross-franchise conclusions by class

### Bronze Saints
The Bronze Saints are the franchise’s emotional core. Their “power level” is intentionally unstable: they are **nominally low rank, narratively top tier**. Seiya, Shiryu, Hyoga, Shun, and Ikki repeatedly punch above class because the series is built on Cosmo breakthroughs, not static RPG tiers [[29]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Cloths)[[65]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya)[[67]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Saint_Seiya_characters).

### Silver Saints
Silver Saints are more structurally important than raw fan discourse sometimes gives them credit for. They are:
- the series’ most common mentor rank,
- the most frequent transition-rank between ordinary and elite,
- and, in cases like **Suikyō** and **Odysseus**, the foundation for some of the franchise’s most dramatic rank changes [[68]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Next_Dimension).

### Gold Saints
Gold Saints are the single most reusable prestige class in the franchise:
- bosses in classic Sanctuary,
- martyrs in Hades,
- protagonists in **Episode G**,
- tragic legends in **Lost Canvas**,
- resurrected headliners in **Soul of Gold**,
- elder symbols in **Omega** [[17]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Episode.G)[[22]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_The_Lost_Canvas)[[74]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Soul_of_Gold)[[75]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya_Omega).

If you asked which class most defines the expanded franchise beyond the original story, the answer is probably **Gold Saints**.

### Marina Generals
The seven Generals are crucial because they establish the franchise template of:
- a god,
- seven elite sub-commanders,
- a symbolic sacred architecture (the sea pillars),
- and one or two morally ambiguous survivors (especially **Kanon** and **Sorrento**) [[84]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Mariners)[[95]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Sea_Dragon_Kanon)[[98]](https://tvtropes.org/pmwiki/pmwiki.php/Characters/SaintSeiyaMariners).

### God Warriors
The God Warriors are mostly an **anime-side mythology**, but an important one. The classic Asgard warriors are tragic manipulated guardians; the **Soul of Gold** set are Yggdrasil-era chamber bosses used to stage one final Gold-Saint epic [[106]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/es/wiki/Arco_de_Asgard)[[86]](https://www.saintseiya-gold.com/en/story.html)[[74]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Soul_of_Gold).

### Specters
The Specters are the largest enemy order and the one most tied to **world structure**. Marina Generals guard pillars; God Warriors guard Asgard; Specters populate an entire **Underworld cosmology**. The Judges, twin gods, and Hades himself form arguably the franchise’s most layered villain hierarchy [[31]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Surplices)[[120]](https://tvtropes.org/pmwiki/pmwiki.php/Characters/SaintSeiyaSpecters)[[124]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_The_Hades_Chapter).

## 11) Bottom line

If you organize *Saint Seiya* by armor classes, the franchise stops looking like one giant unmanageable canon and starts looking like a set of recurring **mythic military systems**:

- **Athena’s Cloths** = the heroic core, with Bronze as miracle-makers and Gold as the prestige elite [[29]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Cloths)[[66]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Saint_Seiya_Episode.G_characters)
- **Poseidon’s Scales** = ancient, aristocratic, sea-temple boss hierarchy [[84]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Mariners)[[30]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Scales)
- **Hades’ Surplices** = vast infernal army with the deepest institutional structure [[31]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Surplices)[[120]](https://tvtropes.org/pmwiki/pmwiki.php/Characters/SaintSeiyaSpecters)
- **Odin’s God Warriors** = anime-side northern elite knight order, tragic in classic Asgard and more systematized in *Soul of Gold* [[106]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/es/wiki/Arco_de_Asgard)[[86]](https://www.saintseiya-gold.com/en/story.html)
- **Spin-off variants** (*Saintias*, *Chronotectors*, *Leaves*, *Cloth Stones*) = ways the franchise keeps reinventing the same core idea: sacred armor as destiny, identity, and rank [[33]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Pallasites)[[34]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Dryades)[[45]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Clothstones)[[71]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_Seiya:_Saintia_Sh%C5%8D)

And in almost every continuity, the same rule remains true:

**Armor sets the ceiling, but Cosmo decides who actually reaches it.** [[29]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Cloths)

If you want, I can turn this into a **full character encyclopedia table** next—grouped by **Bronze / Silver / Gold / Marina / God Warrior / Specter**, with one row per character and columns for **continuity, armor, techniques, arcs, and fate**.
